Title: The basal transcription factor II H subunit Tfb5 is required for stress response and pathogenicity in the tangerine pathotype of Alternaria alternata

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basal transcription factor IIH subunit Tfb5; TFIIH complex subunit
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ACT toxin; melanin; cutinase-associated cuticle degradation products
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Host-selective toxin; pigment (melanin); cuticle-degrading enzyme activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture filtrate for ACT toxin; fungal mycelia for melanin; extracellular culture filtrate for cutinase activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ACT toxin is required for pathogenicity on citrus; melanin contributes to UV protection; cutinase supports host penetration and virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Aatfb5 positively regulates ACT toxin production, melanin accumulation, and cutinase activity, thereby promoting virulence-related metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; genetic complementation; HPLC toxin assay; melanin quantification; cutinase activity assay; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of Aatfb5 reduced ACT toxin production and melanin accumulation. 2. Aatfb5 loss lowered cutinase activity and down-regulated two cutinase genes, impairing virulence. 3. Transcriptome analysis showed Aatfb5 broadly regulates secondary metabolism, oxidative phosphorylation, peroxisome-related genes, and stress-response metabolism.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced during infection of citrus leaves, increasing about 20-fold at 3 hpi and 30-fold at 6 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during infection of citrus leaves, increasing about 20-fold at 3 hpi and 30-fold at 6 hpi
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Citrus leaf infection; UV irradiation; DNA-damaging agents; oxidants; cell wall-interfering agents
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous Aatfb5 promoter (native) for GFP fusion/complementation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target because disrupting Aatfb5 simultaneously reduces toxin biosynthesis, cutinase production, stress tolerance, and virulence; not a crop engineering target
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion also reduces radial growth, sporulation, pigmentation, surface hydrophobicity, and cell wall integrity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Aatfb5 physically interacts with Aatfb2 and likely functions within TFIIH; transcriptomics suggested positive effects on peroxisome-associated functions and negative effects on oxidative phosphorylation; discussion also notes possible involvement in micronutrient metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ester bonds in plant cutin, contributing to cuticle degradation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream extracellular degradation step associated with host penetration
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functionally contributory and partially redundant with AaCut15
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-gene deletion; double-gene deletion with Aacut15; cutinase activity assay; virulence assay; transcript profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Aacut2 promotes cutinase activity and full virulence on citrus leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aacut2 expression was down-regulated in ΔAatfb5. 2. Single deletion of Aacut2 slightly reduced virulence. 3. Combined deletion with Aacut15 greatly reduced cutinase activity and lesion size, indicating partially redundant virulence functions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular culture filtrate / host infection interface
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Facilitates host cuticle penetration and fungal virulence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induced assay conditions using modified Czapek's medium amended with tomato skins for cutinase measurement
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing penetration and virulence; likely most effective when combined with AaCut15
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single deletion had little or no effect on growth, conidiation, or ACT toxin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with AaCut15; single deletion had little effect on overall cutinase activity compared with double deletion

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ester bonds in plant cutin, contributing to cuticle degradation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream extracellular degradation step associated with host penetration
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functionally contributory and partially redundant with AaCut2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-gene deletion; double-gene deletion with Aacut2; cutinase activity assay; virulence assay; transcript profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Aacut15 promotes cutinase activity and full virulence on citrus leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aacut15 expression was down-regulated in ΔAatfb5. 2. Single deletion of Aacut15 slightly reduced virulence. 3. Combined deletion with Aacut2 greatly reduced cutinase activity and lesion size, indicating partially redundant virulence functions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular culture filtrate / host infection interface
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Facilitates host cuticle penetration and fungal virulence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induced assay conditions using modified Czapek's medium amended with tomato skins for cutinase measurement
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing penetration and virulence; likely most effective when combined with AaCut2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single deletion had little or no effect on growth, conidiation, or ACT toxin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with AaCut2; single deletion had little effect on overall cutinase activity compared with double deletion

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Basal transcription factor; TFIIH complex subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of multiple metabolic/virulence genes within the TFIIH basal transcription machinery
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary metabolite biosynthesis; toxin biosynthesis; melanin biosynthesis; cutinase production; oxidative stress response; peroxisome-associated metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Aacut2; Aacut15; pksA (AALT_g6058); ACTTS3; metalloprotease-coding gene in ACT toxin cluster; AaTrr1; AaTsa1; AaGlr1; AaGpx3; Aapex24; catalase genes AALT_g1664, AALT_g1666, AALT_g1523
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or maintains expression of Aacut2, Aacut15, pksA, ACTTS3, metalloprotease gene, AaTrr1, AaTsa1, AaGlr1, AaGpx3, Aapex24, and catalase genes; negatively associated with expression of several oxidative phosphorylation genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Aatfb5 is a decisive positive regulator of virulence-related metabolite production, especially ACT toxin, melanin, and cutinase-associated penetration functions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aatfb5 deletion reduced ACT toxin production, melanin biosynthesis, and cutinase activity, causing severe loss of virulence. 2. RNA-seq showed Aatfb5 globally regulates secondary metabolite clusters and stress-response metabolic genes. 3. Aatfb5 expression is strongly induced during infection and the protein interacts with Aatfb2, supporting a TFIIH-mediated regulatory role.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted deletion and complementation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; HPLC metabolite analysis; enzyme activity assays; virulence assays; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ACT toxin; melanin; cutinase-associated cuticle degradation products
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Host-selective toxin; pigment; cuticle degradation-related products
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture filtrate for ACT toxin; fungal mycelia for melanin; extracellular milieu/host surface for cutinase action
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ACT toxin drives pathogenicity on citrus; melanin protects against UV; cutinase enables host penetration and full virulence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator required for full production of ACT toxin and for expression of cutinase genes needed for virulence
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Infection of citrus leaves induces Aatfb5 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated during citrus infection, peaking at 3-6 hpi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated during citrus infection, peaking at 3-6 hpi
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as part of the TFIIH complex; physically interacts with Aatfb2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Citrus infection; UV light; DNA-damaging agents; oxidants; cell wall stress agents
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate antifungal intervention point because it coordinately controls toxin synthesis, oxidative stress tolerance, and penetration-associated metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption also affects fungal growth, sporulation, pigmentation, and stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Aatfb5 is not an enzyme in the ACT or melanin pathways but a global basal transcription factor required for expression of multiple metabolism-related genes; Aatfb2 appeared essential because deletion mutants could not be obtained
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous Aatfb5 promoter (native) for GFP fusion/complementation
